basics.name: James Zhang
basics.label: Pre-Doctoral Research Fellow, Yale School of Management

work.name: Yale School of Management
work.summary: Advised by Bryan Kelly, Head of Machine Learning at AQR.
highlights: Benchmarking whether frontier large language models replicate expert-level investor judgment, reading Dow Jones Newswires articles to classify the relevant global futures markets.
Building a dynamic macroeconomic knowledge graph from financial news to study Shiller's 'excess volatility' puzzle, linking the flow of macro information to asset prices.
Building 'Global Macroeconomic Data', a panel of returns for 92 global futures contracts (LSEG Tick History, LSEG Datastream, Compustat).

work.name: Kalshi — Independent Trading
work.summary: Automated market-making on Kalshi prediction markets, built in Rust.
highlights: Deploying a market-making system across WTI crude oil, Bitcoin, and other commodity markets with real-time position tracking and risk controls.
Pricing, volatility modeling, and delta hedging from live CME Globex MDP 3.0 options data (Databento).
Quoting same-game parlays priced off FanDuel odds into live request-for-quote auctions across NBA, WNBA, MLB, and World Cup markets.

work.name: Amazon
highlights: Built real-time performance analytics in Java surfacing key metrics to over 2 million active Amazon Sellers.
Led a database migration from Elasticsearch to AWS Timestream, projected to cut costs 10% and user latency 5%.
Received a full-time return offer (Video Shopping Experiences team).

work.name: University of Maryland, College Park
highlights: Designed a class of latent three-dimensional 'Tensor Factor Models' capturing drivers of asset returns across time, lags, and the cross-section; implemented in Python on 96 monthly factor portfolios.
Implemented GPU-accelerated Orthogonalized Alternating Least Squares and PARAFAC algorithms in Jax for high-dimensional firm-characteristics data.

work.name: Capital One
highlights: Built a FastAPI Slackbot for the Data Remediation team supporting internal data-lake workflows; improved query speeds 15%.

work.name: Northrop Grumman
highlights: Developed a time-varying atmospheric attenuation model with Chebyshev polynomials and approximation theory in PyTorch.
Built a platform to generate atmospheric attenuation reference tables using Pandas and the Modtran C++ API.

education.area: First-year PhD coursework: Financial Economics I, Econometrics I
Computer Science & Mathematics; Minor in Computational Finance
